CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value INTEGER);
SELECT rnd(10) AS random_num FROM test_table WHERE id < 5;
SELECT (SELECT rnd(100) FROM test_table WHERE id = 1) AS random_value_1, (SELECT rnd(100) FROM test_table WHERE id = 2) AS random_value_2;
CREATE TABLE second_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, ref_id INTEGER, FOREIGN KEY(ref_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
SELECT t1.value, rnd(10) AS random_num FROM test_table t1 JOIN second_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.ref_id WHERE t1.value > rnd(5);
SELECT rnd(5) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT value FROM test_table) AS subquery WHERE value < rnd(10);
CREATE TABLE third_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, random_value INTEGER DEFAULT rnd(50));
SELECT AVG(random_value) FROM third_table WHERE random_value > rnd(25);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE rnd(5) = value;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT rnd(3) FROM test_table);
